evals.name: explore-multisheet-file
evals.expected_output: A summary of the file: two sheets (Sales and Summary), the Sales sheet has 6 rows including a header with Rep, Q1–Q4 columns, and the Summary sheet has 3 rows. Key data values should be mentioned.
assertions: Response mentions both sheet names: Sales and Summary
Response describes the columns in the Sales sheet (Rep, Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4)
Response includes at least some of the actual data values (e.g. rep names or numbers)
Response does not hallucinate sheet names or columns that don't exist

evals.name: precise-cell-lookup
evals.prompt: In evals/files/sales.ods, what is the value in cell C3 of the Sales sheet?
evals.expected_output: The value 15200 (Carol's Q1 — row 3, column C).
assertions: Response states the value 15200
Response identifies the correct cell (C3) and sheet (Sales)

evals.name: append-rows
evals.prompt: Add a new sales rep to evals/files/sales.ods — Frank with Q1=10500, Q2=11800, Q3=13200, Q4=15600. Add him to the Sales sheet.
evals.expected_output: Frank is appended as a new row in the Sales sheet. The file is saved and the new row is confirmed.
assertions: Response confirms the row was added successfully
The Sales sheet in the output file contains a row with Frank and the values 10500, 11800, 13200, 15600
No existing rows in the Sales sheet were modified

evals.name: sheet-management
evals.prompt: In evals/files/sales.ods, rename the Summary sheet to Overview and add a new empty sheet called Notes.
evals.expected_output: Summary is renamed to Overview and a new sheet called Notes is added. The Sales sheet is untouched.
assertions: The output file has a sheet named Overview
The output file has a sheet named Notes
The output file no longer has a sheet named Summary
The Sales sheet still exists with its original data intact

evals.name: create-new-file
evals.prompt: Create a new ODS file at /tmp/budget.ods with two sheets: Income and Expenses.
evals.expected_output: A new ODS file created at /tmp/budget.ods with empty Income and Expenses sheets.
assertions: Response confirms the file was created
The created file contains exactly two sheets named Income and Expenses

evals.name: missing-email-identification
evals.prompt: Look at evals/files/contacts.ods and tell me which contacts are missing an email address.
evals.expected_output: Bob and David are missing emails. The response should identify them by name.
assertions: Response identifies Bob as missing an email
Response identifies David as missing an email
Response does not incorrectly flag Alice, Carol, or Eve as missing emails
Response states the count: 2 contacts are missing emails

evals.name: nonexistent-sheet-error
evals.expected_output: A clear error that the sheet 'Revenue' does not exist, along with the names of sheets that do exist.
assertions: Response communicates that the sheet was not found
Response lists the actual available sheet names (Sales, Summary)
